{{Short description="Particle with integer spin that obeys Bose–Einstein statistics}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Boson
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quantum mechanics]], [[Spin (physics)]], [[Identical particles]], [[Bose–Einstein statistics]]
| spin = Integer (0, 1, 2, …)
| statistics = [[Bose–Einstein statistics]]
| examples = [[Photon]], [[Gluon]], [[W and Z bosons]], [[Higgs boson]]
}}

In [[particle physics]], a '''boson''' is a [[subatomic particle]] that obeys [[Bose–Einstein statistics]] and has integer [[spin (physics)|spin]] (<math>0, 1, 2, \ldots</math>). Bosons are named after [[Satyendra Nath Bose]], who (with [[Albert Einstein]]) developed the statistical description for such particles.

Unlike [[fermion]]s, multiple bosons can occupy the same [[quantum state]]. This property leads to phenomena such as [[Bose–Einstein condensation]] and [[laser]] emission.

== Types ==

=== Gauge bosons (force carriers) ===
Spin-1 bosons that mediate [[fundamental interaction]]s:
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Boson !! Force !! Mass
|-
| [[Photon]] (γ) || [[Electromagnetism]] || 0
|-
| [[Gluon]] (g) × 8 || [[Strong interaction]] || 0
|-
| [[W and Z bosons|W⁺, W⁻, Z⁰]] || [[Weak interaction]] || 80.4, 91.2 GeV/c²
|}

=== Scalar bosons ===
Spin-0 bosons:
* [[Higgs boson]] (<math>H^0</math>) — responsible for mass generation via the [[Higgs mechanism]]; mass ~125 GeV/c²; discovered in 2012 at the [[Large Hadron Collider]]

=== Hypothetical bosons ===
* [[Graviton]] — hypothetical spin-2 boson mediating [[gravity]]
* [[Axion]] — hypothetical particle solving the strong CP problem

=== Composite bosons ===
Bound states with integer total spin:
* [[Meson]]s — quark–antiquark pairs (pions, kaons)
* [[Helium-4]] atoms
* [[Cooper pair]]s — electron pairs in [[superconductor]]s

== Bose–Einstein condensation ==
{{Main|Bose–Einstein condensate}}
At very low temperatures, a macroscopic number of bosons occupy the ground state, forming a [[Bose–Einstein condensate]] — a novel state of matter with macroscopic quantum coherence.

== Bose–Einstein statistics ==
{{Main|Bose–Einstein statistics}}
The average occupation number for bosons:
:<math>\langle n \rangle = \frac{1}{e^{(E - \mu)/k_B T} - 1}</math>

There is no limit on occupation — bosons "like" to be in the same state.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Bose–Einstein statistics]]
* [[Fermion]]
* [[Higgs boson]]
* [[Identical particles]]
* [[Spin (physics)]]
* [[Standard Model]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Bosons| ]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
[[Category:Quantum field theory]]
